input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** `dotnet workload search` shows required platform versions; Xcode release notes list supported iOS SDKs. Match the iOS SDK version MAUI targets.
- **Post-install:** Launch Xcode once to accept the license, then:
  ```
  xcode-select --install          # command-line tools
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version` and `xcrun simctl list runtimes`

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest **LTS or STS** SDK that supports MAUI (currently .NET 8 LTS or .NET 9).
- **Version discovery:** The [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) specify the minimum SDK version per MAUI release.
- **Install:** Use the official `.pkg` installer.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version` and `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```
- This pulls in `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, and their dependencies.
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — you should see `maui` (or the individual platform workloads).

## 4. Android SDK / JDK

The MAUI workload installs the **Android SDK** and a compatible **JDK (Microsoft OpenJDK)** automatically. If you need manual control:

- **Android Studio (optional):** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) — useful for the AVD manager and SDK manager UI.
- **Environment variables** (add to `~/.zshrc`):
  ```bash
  export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
  export JAVA_HOME="/usr/libexec/java_home"
  export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$PATH"
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies \
    -f net8.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$ANDROID_HOME" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
  Or: `adb --version` and `emulator -list-avds`

## 5. Create & Run a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Android (emulator — start one first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 6. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | Lightweight, free |
| **JetBrains Rider** | Full-featured, paid |

*(Visual Studio for Mac has been retired.)*

## 7. End-to-End Verification Checklist

```bash
xcodebuild -version            # Xcode + CLI tools
dotnet --list-sdks              # .NET SDK present
dotnet workload list            # maui workload installed
adb devices                     # Android toolchain reachable
dotnet new maui -n SmokeTest && cd SmokeTest
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios      # iOS compiles
dotnet build -f net8.0-android  # Android compiles
```

If all six commands succeed, your environment is fully operational. Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you target .NET 9.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking for a comprehensive setup guide for .NET MAUI development on macOS targeting iOS and Android. This is a knowledge/advice question - no code changes or file operations needed. I should provide a clear, structured plan based on my knowledge of the .NET MAUI ecosystem.

Let me give a thorough but well-organized answer covering all the requirements.
